assertion_results.text: The remediation output describes the digest protocol for the upstream backport task: re-fetching the task description from Jira after create_issue and computing a SHA-256 digest using scripts/sha256-digest.py (§1.66)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 86-97: 'After creating the upstream backport task, the following description digest procedure is performed before creating issue links or any other comments: 1. Re-fetch the task description from the Jira API after create_issue returns the new task key... upstream_desc = jira.get_issue(<upstream-task-key>, fields=["description"]) 2. Write the re-fetched description to a temp file and compute the SHA-256 digest using scripts/sha256-digest.py: python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/task-desc.md'

assertion_results.text: The remediation output describes the digest protocol for the downstream propagation subtask: re-fetching the task description from Jira after create_issue and computing a SHA-256 digest using scripts/sha256-digest.py (§1.66)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 183-195: 'After creating the downstream propagation subtask, the following description digest procedure is performed before creating issue links or any other comments: 1. Re-fetch the task description from the Jira API after create_issue returns the new task key... downstream_desc = jira.get_issue(<downstream-task-key>, fields=["description"]) 2. Write the re-fetched description to a temp file and compute the SHA-256 digest using scripts/sha256-digest.py: python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/task-desc.md'

assertion_results.text: The remediation output includes the digest comment marker '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' in the described steps for each remediation task — both upstream and downstream (§1.66)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 98-99 (upstream): 'Post a standalone digest comment on the newly created task with the marker [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' and line 198-199 (downstream): 'Post a standalone digest comment on the newly created task with the marker [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:'. Both include ADF examples with text '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>'.

assertion_results.text: The remediation output sequences digest comments BEFORE issue links (Depend, Blocks) or other comments in the described procedure for each task (§1.66, shared/description-digest-protocol.md Rules)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 122: 'Only after the digest comment is posted, proceed to create issue links and other comments on this task.' for upstream. Line 220: 'Only after the digest comment is posted, proceed to create issue links and other comments on this task.' for downstream. The 'Complete Procedure Sequence' section (lines 228-249) explicitly shows: upstream task steps 1-4 (create, re-fetch, compute, digest comment) before step 5 (create_link), and downstream steps 6-9 (create, re-fetch, compute, digest comment) before steps 10-11 (create_link). Line 249: 'the description digest comment (steps 4 and 9) is posted before issue links (steps 5, 10, 11)'

assertion_results.text: The remediation output specifies that the digest is computed from the re-fetched description (via Jira API after create_issue), not from the description string passed to create_issue (shared/description-digest-protocol.md Hashing)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 87-88 (upstream): 'Re-fetch the task description from the Jira API after create_issue returns the new task key... This is critical because Jira normalizes content during storage -- the description passed to create_issue may differ from what Jira stores'. Lines 184-185 (downstream): 'The digest must be computed from the re-fetched description, not from the description string passed to create_issue, because Jira normalizes content during storage'. Line 251: 'Do not hash the description string passed to create_issue -- always re-fetch from the API after creation, since Jira normalizes content during storage.'
